perturbation.original_text: In accordance with Rule 13d-1(k) under the Securities Exchange Act of 1934, as amended, the undersigned hereby confirm the agreement by and among them to the joint filing on behalf of them of the Statement on Schedule 13D and any and all further amendments thereto, with respect to the securities of the above referenced issuer, and that this Agreement be included as an Exhibit to such filing.
perturbation.changed_text: In accordance with Rule 13d-1(k) under the Securities Exchange Act of 1934, as amended, the undersigned hereby confirm the agreement by and among them to the joint filing on behalf of them of the Statement on Schedule 13D and any and all further amendments thereto, with respect to the securities of the above referenced issuer, and that this Agreement may or may not be included as an Exhibit to such filing, as determined by the involved parties.
perturbation.explanation: This change introduces uncertainty by stating that the Joint Filing Agreement may or may not be included as an exhibit, which contradicts the original intent to include it. This undermines the purpose of the agreement and its formal incorporation into the filing. This is an In-Text contradiction because it is contradicting itself in the same section.
perturbation.contradicted_text: In accordance with Rule 13d-1(k) under the Securities Exchange Act of 1934, as amended, the undersigned hereby confirm the agreement by and among them to the joint filing on behalf of them of the Statement on Schedule 13D and any and all further amendments thereto, with respect to the securities of the above referenced issuer, and that this Agreement may or may not be included as an Exhibit to such filing, as determined by the involved parties. This Agreement may be executed in any number of counterparts each of which shall be deemed to be an original and all of which together shall be deemed to constitute one and the same Agreement.
perturbation.justification: The original text mandates the inclusion of the Joint Filing Agreement as an exhibit. The changed text introduces uncertainty by stating that the agreement 'may or may not' be included, directly contradicting the original intent. The contradicted text is actually the changed text, and the change from 'shall' to 'may or may not' directly breaks the compatibility between the intended purpose of the section and the section itself. This creates a structural flaw - in text contradiction as the clause now has conflicting implications, which undermines the agreement and its formal incorporation into the filing.

perturbation.original_text: This Agreement may be executed in any number of counterparts each of which shall be deemed to be an original and all of which together shall be deemed to constitute one and the same Agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: This Agreement must be executed in only one counterpart which shall be deemed to be the original agreement.
perturbation.explanation: The change restricts the agreement to a single counterpart, contradicting the initial allowance for multiple counterparts and potentially invalidating versions held by different parties. This creates an In-Text contradiction, because in a Joint Filing Agreement each party might have a copy. This is contradicting its very nature.
perturbation.contradicted_text: In accordance with Rule 13d-1(k) under the Securities Exchange Act of 1934, as amended, the undersigned hereby confirm the agreement by and among them to the joint filing on behalf of them of the Statement on Schedule 13D and any and all further amendments thereto, with respect to the securities of the above referenced issuer, and that this Agreement be included as an Exhibit to such filing. This Agreement must be executed in only one counterpart which shall be deemed to be the original agreement.
perturbation.justification: The changed text mandates that the agreement must be executed in only one counterpart, while the original text allows for multiple counterparts. This directly contradicts the nature of a Joint Filing Agreement where multiple parties are involved and might each hold a copy of the agreement. The contradicted text contains the changed text making this a strong in-text contradiction, because having only one original conflicts with the joint filing nature of the agreement.

perturbation.original_text: IN WITNESS WHEREOF, the undersigned hereby execute this Agreement as of July 6, 2020.
perturbation.changed_text: IN WITNESS WHEREOF, the undersigned may execute this Agreement at their convenience, or may choose not to execute it at all.
perturbation.explanation: Changing "hereby execute" to "may execute... or may choose not to execute it at all" makes the execution of the agreement optional, which contradicts the initial statement that the undersigned 'confirm the agreement'. This casts doubt on whether all parties are bound by the agreement. By making execution optional, it defeats the purpose of a binding joint filing agreement, which by definition, requires execution by all involved parties.
perturbation.contradicted_text: In accordance with Rule 13d-1(k) under the Securities Exchange Act of 1934, as amended, the undersigned hereby confirm the agreement by and among them to the joint filing on behalf of them of the Statement on Schedule 13D and any and all further amendments thereto, with respect to the securities of the above referenced issuer, and that this Agreement be included as an Exhibit to such filing.
perturbation.justification: The changed text introduces an optional execution of the agreement, stating parties "may execute this Agreement at their convenience, or may choose not to execute it at all." This directly contradicts the original text, which stated the parties "hereby execute this Agreement". It also creates a strong contradiction with the Contradicted Text, which states that "the undersigned hereby confirm the agreement". By making execution optional, it nullifies the confirmation of the agreement and thus creates a strong and in-text contradiction. This falls under Structural Flaws - In Text Contradiction as the modification defeats the purpose of a binding joint filing agreement.
